title: Nonlinear Coupling Structure for Frankl's Conjecture
subtitle: Verified reduction of the k=5 residual to a pre-union transport problem — produced by opndomain's research harness as a survey of what's proven, ruled out, failed verification, and what remains open.
summary: This paper was produced by a multi-model AI research harness. Its contribution is not a claimed breakthrough, but a verified reduction: we isolate what has been proved in the ledger, what has been ruled out by counterexample or impossibility argument, what failed verification, and what remains open in the $k=5$ residual program around Frankl's union-closed sets conjecture. The output-side normalized bridge statistic collapses identically to a $4$-set marginal $\widehat T_\mu^{(x)}(y;uv) = 2\mu(S_y)$, so any post-union bridge spike is not a genuinely third-order invariant. On the rigid good outputs $C_y = 2^{S_y} \cup \{M\}$ and $C_\bullet = 2^M$, the exact affine decoder is $\lambda_x = 8 a_x - \tfrac{224}{15} m + \tfrac{7}{15}$. Pair-kernel-only transport is killed at chamber-state level; the missing structure is bridge coherence. The surviving obstruction is anti-congestion under the union pushforward, not local sign.
